children: (click or press space to speed up)
(this search is fake - don't tell anyone)
> awaiting input... (run a cheat above - it applies & saves instantly)
A creature mod caused the game to crash. It has been automatically disabled. Click the button below to reload - your save data is intact.
A curated shop that restocks every day. Grab grading finds, flip stock, and the occasional high-end headliner.

A full binder system is on the way. Create and customize your own binders, fill them with your cards, and organize your collection however you like - by set, rarity, personal picks, or any theme you want.
Bundles take 90% of summed value but pay fees once. Better for stacks of low-value commons.
Buy low. Sell high. Pay weird fees.
Buyers clicked Buy. Some pay quickly, some slow-walk it, some bail entirely.
Cards you've posted online at your chosen prices. Buyers nibble probabilistically - list close to value for steady sales, list above for jackpot odds.
Chase print - collect one to reveal
Earned by making sales. Unlocks new marketplaces as you grow your business.
Every creature grouped by set. Click any creature to see all its prints.
Expires back to inventory if no buyer. Lower price = faster sale.
Export downloads a JSON of all creatures. Edit names, stats, and tags - or add new entries - then import to apply. Reload required.
Export downloads a backup file. Importing replaces your current game.
Fictional brands only. No real cards harmed.
Gathering data - the chart fills in as you play.
Grade your gems for big payout multipliers - just mind the weird fees.
Graded slabs always choose between SlabHub and FeeBay when you sell them - the primary store applies to raw cards.
Hover any underlined term in the game for the same definition inline.
Hunt cheap listings on the Marketplace and flip them for profit.
In showcase - actions locked. Click the crown to remove.
Lifetime totals, reputation progress, and where the next milestones live.
Listed online - see Online Listings tab.
Live stock history - every card your team has bought in and flipped back out.
No bids yet. Be the first - or wait for the goblins to circle.
No employees yet. Hire an automation team to flip cards and watch the profit roll in.
No grades received yet. Send your first card.
No listings here. Try refreshing or another marketplace.
No sales yet. When something sells, it'll show up here.
No spare stock. Buy or source cards, then put your best ones in the cases. Showcased cards stay in the Collection.
No stock on the shelf. Cards your Scouts source land here until a Flipper sells them - pull anything you'd rather keep for yourself.
No trends right now. Markets are quiet.
No walk-ins yet. Put cards on display and customers will start buying.
Note: this is a temporary testing interface. A proper modding system is planned - this JSON import method will be sunset when that ships.
Nothing in grading. Go to Inventory and submit a sharp card.
Nothing's moved through yet - hire a Scout and a Flipper, then watch the belt fill up.
Press Enter to breach /admin - you didn't see this.
Promote to the next business level over on the Upgrades tab.
Promote your business to unlock new tiers of tools - every level reveals stronger upgrades.
Reviews help real players find FeeBay and keep development alive. Every single one counts.
Saves a timestamped copy to a folder on your PC.
Sell at a profit to gain reputation. New marketplaces unlock automatically.
Shipping, protection and prep the company eats on every card it sells.
Skip tutorial - only if you know what you're doing
Snappy timed auctions. Read the room, time your bids, don't feed the goblin.
Tags pumping or dumping right now. Time the market or get rugged.
Tags you hold inventory in - highlighted when a trend is active.
Team full - promote your business for more slots
The lights at the post office shut off. Time to count the day's haul.
There's a small chance (~15%) the card is damaged as the case comes apart, dropping it to Damaged condition.
Three graders. Different costs, speeds, premiums, and chaos levels. Pick your fighter.
You hold the high bid. Sit tight - or arm a proxy max and let the goblin sweat.
Your spendable balance. Used to buy cards, pay grading fees, and purchase upgrades.
a big raise can scare a sweating bidder out
this console is testing-only. cheats apply immediately and persist to your save.

desc: How loud the sound effects play.
Show the end-of-day report each time a day rolls over.
Switch the whole game between light and dark.
definition: A bundle of 3-8 random cards sold at a single price. You don't see what's inside until you open it.
A card that has been professionally graded and sealed in a tamper-proof plastic case. Slabs sell for more than the raw card.
A card that has not been professionally graded. Cheaper than a slab of the same card, but the buyer has to trust your condition call.
A chase card that's not part of the printed set list - a hidden bonus pull. Always valuable, especially from sealed lots.
A grab bag of pre-graded slabs from mixed grading companies. Quality varies wildly - a bucket of dice rolls.
A larger bundle - usually 8-15 cards - pulled straight from a binder. More cards than a mystery lot, slightly worse hit rate.
A raw card that looks clean enough to potentially come back as a Gem Mint 10 if graded. A grading bet, not a guarantee.
An offer well below the asking price. Clueless sellers sometimes accept; hyped sellers rage-reject.
How perfectly the art is centered inside the card border. Off-center cards lose points at grading even if the surface is flawless.
The biggest gamble on the marketplace: 15-35 random cards inherited from a storage auction. Lots of bulk, occasional bangers.
The chance this listing is a counterfeit card. High-risk listings can still be deals - but the card may be worthless if you ignore it.
The top rarity tier on most cards. Significantly rarer (and pricier) than Rare or Holo Rare.
tagline: Auctions and listings. Fees included whether you like it or not.
Card collections. Storage units. Pickup behind the gas station.
Convention booths, dedicated grading runs, sponsorships.
Fast and cheap. Slightly less hype on the resale.
Influencers say BUY. Then they say SELL. Then they say BUY again.
Members only. Mythic and Prototype slabs change hands here.
Posts shipped from the garage at 11pm.
Premium. Expensive. Slower. Highest market premium.
Storefront in a strip mall. The OPEN sign blinks.
The auction goblin is watching. Bid wisely.
They write magazine features about you.
Two employees, a glass case, and a backroom safe.
Wildly inconsistent. Occasionally gifts you a gem. Occasionally mislabels.
You and a cardboard box of binders.
Your neighbor is selling a binder. Get there first.
